name: Port of La Puebla and Atazar Reservoir — circular via Somosierra
name_original: Puerto de La Puebla y Embalse de Atazar — circular por la Somosierra
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Retiendas traverse the Sierra Norte de Guadalajara, a region characterized by open landscapes, quiet roads, and varying elevations. The area features the picturesque Jarama River, which carves through the landscape, forming a canyon known as the "Hoz del río Jarama." Cyclists will encounter diverse terrain, from gentle hills to routes with significant elevation gains, often passing through fields and offering panoramic views of the river valley. The region also includes villages showcasing the distinctive "Arquitectura Negra" style. Best road cycling routes around Retiendas The most popular road cycling route is Sierra del Rincón Biosphere Reserve Loop, a 66.9 miles (107.7 km) trail that takes 6 hours 10 minutes to complete. This route offers varied terrain through a protected natural area. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Port of La Puebla and Atazar Reservoir — circular via Somosierra, a difficult 94.1 miles (151.4 km) path. This route includes significant climbs and views of the Atazar Reservoir. Local road cyclists also love the Black Architecture Villages Loop, a 27.1 miles (43.6 km) trail leading through villages with distinctive slate and stone buildings, often completed in about 2 hours 18 minutes. Road cycling around Retiendas is defined by the Jarama River valley, "Arquitectura Negra" villages, and open landscapes with gentle hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Retiendas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from a few reviews. More than 100 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Retiendas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Retiendas?
A: There are over 7 road cycling routes in the Retiendas area, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. These routes traverse the Sierra Norte de Guadalajara, known for its open landscapes and quiet roads.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes near Retiendas?
A: The terrain around Retiendas is diverse, featuring everything from gentle hills to routes with significant elevation gains. You'll encounter picturesque landscapes along the Jarama River valley, fields, and panoramic views. Many roads are well-paved and less-trafficked, providing a pleasant cycling experience.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of road cycling routes around Retiendas?
A: Road cycling routes in Retiendas range from moderate to difficult. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1375081285/black-architecture-villages-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Black Architecture Villages Loop&lt;/a&gt; is considered moderate, while routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017119476/sierra-del-rincon-biosphere-reserve-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra del Rincón Biosphere Reserve Loop&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017110366/port-of-la-puebla-and-atazar-reservoir-circular-via-somosierra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Port of La Puebla and Atazar Reservoir — circular via Somosierra&lt;/a&gt; offer more challenging climbs.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes in the Retiendas area?
A: Yes, many routes around Retiendas are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017110366/port-of-la-puebla-and-atazar-reservoir-circular-via-somosierra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Port of La Puebla and Atazar Reservoir — circular via Somosierra&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1375081285/black-architecture-villages-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Black Architecture Villages Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which are popular choices for exploring the region.

Q: What natural features and landmarks can I see while road cycling near Retiendas?
A: You can explore a variety of natural and historical attractions. The Jarama River carves a dramatic canyon, the "Hoz del río Jarama," offering scenic views. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5098716" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aljibe Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5141678" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Enchanted City of Tamajón&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6346346" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Balagares or La Ventana Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features impressive caves like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6552585" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Prehistoric Cave of Los Torrejones&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any interesting historical sites or villages to visit along the routes?
A: Absolutely. The region is home to the distinctive "Arquitectura Negra" (Black Architecture) villages, characterized by slate and stone buildings. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1375081285/black-architecture-villages-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Black Architecture Villages Loop&lt;/a&gt; specifically takes you through some of these charming towns. You can also visit the impressive 12th-century Monasterio de Bonaval and the Medieval Bridge of Valdesotos, often included in routes along the Jarama river canyon.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Retiendas?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Cyclists frequently praise the quiet roads, the varied terrain suitable for different fitness levels, and the stunning panoramic views of the Jarama river valley and the Sierra Norte de Guadalajara.

Q: Is Retiendas suitable for family-friendly road cycling?
A: While the region offers routes from moderate to difficult, the presence of quiet roads and varying elevations means that some sections or shorter routes could be suitable for families with older children or those comfortable with moderate climbs. It's best to check individual route profiles for specific elevation and distance details to ensure they match your family's capabilities.

Q: Can I find cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the road cycling routes?
A: Retiendas and the surrounding "Arquitectura Negra" villages are charming rural communities. While specific cafes are not detailed in the route information, these villages often have local establishments where cyclists can stop for refreshments. It's advisable to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially on longer routes, as services might be spread out.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Retiendas?
A: The Sierra Norte de Guadalajara offers pleasant conditions for road cycling during spring and autumn, when temperatures are milder. Summer can be warm, especially in the middle of the day, while winter might bring colder temperatures and potentially some snow at higher elevations. Checking local weather forecasts before your ride is always recommended.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints or scenic spots along the routes?
A: Yes, the region is rich in scenic spots. The routes often provide panoramic views of the Jarama river valley. A notable viewpoint is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6346346" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Balagares or La Ventana Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. Additionally, the dramatic "Hoz del río Jarama" canyon itself offers exceptional vistas along its path.

Q: What is the longest road cycling route available around Retiendas?
A: One of the longest and most challenging routes is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017110366/port-of-la-puebla-and-atazar-reservoir-circular-via-somosierra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Port of La Puebla and Atazar Reservoir — circular via Somosierra&lt;/a&gt;, which spans 94.1 miles (151.4 km) and includes significant climbs, offering a demanding yet rewarding experience.
